THIS WAS THE FIRST EF-1 TORNADO NORTHWEST OF GRANT PARK. THIS TRACKED APPROXIMATELY FOUR MILES TO THE EAST-NORTHEAST WITH MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 100-110 MPH. THIS HAD A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 200 YARDS. THIS HEAVILY DAMAGED/DESTROYED BARNS AND CAUSED WIDESPREAD SNAPPING OF HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES. THIS WAS REVISED FOR START POINT AND PATH LENGTH.

FIRST EF-1 TORNADO NORTHWEST OF GRANT PARK TRACKED APPROXIMATELY 4.5 MILES TO THE EAST-NORTHEAST AND HAD MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 100-110 MPH. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 200 YARDS. CAUSED WIDESPREAD SNAPPING OF HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES...AND HEAVILY DAMAGED/DESTROYED BARNS.

SECOND EF-1 TORNADO NORTHWEST OF GRANT PARK ALSO HAD A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF ABOUT 200 YARDS. WIDESPREAD SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE...INCLUDING NUMEROUS HARDWOODS AND SOFTWOODS SNAPPED AT THE TRUNK. A FAIRLY WELL BUILT SHED WAS RIPPED FROM CEMENT PILINGS...TOSSED AND DESTROYED AND THE PILINGS WERE ALSO TOSSED UP TO 40 FEET. MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS WERE 100-110 MPH.

THIS WAS THE SECOND EF-1 TORNADO NORTHWEST OF GRANT PARK. THIS HAD A PATH LENGTH OF APPROXIMATELY 4.5 MILES WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100 MPH AND MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 300 YARDS. THIS TORNADO PRODUCED WIDESPREAD SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE...INCLUDING NUMEROUS HARDWOODS AND SOFTWOODS SNAPPED AT THE TRUNK. AN OUTBUILDING WAS ALSO PARTIALLY DAMAGED. THIS WAS REVISED FOR START POINT AND PATH LENGTH.

EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH UP TO 200 YARDS NORTHEAST OF GRANT PARK. PATH ENDED ABOUT 5 MILES WEST SOUTHWEST OF LOWELL IN LAKE COUNTY INDIANA. MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE INCLUDED STAINED GLASS WINDOWS BLOWN OUT OF A CHURCH BY LARGE LIMBS...TREE BRANCH IMPALED INTO CHURCH AND A HOME...SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO A HOME UNDER CONSTRUCTION BUT SEALED...HARDWOODS AND SOFTWOODS SNAPPED AT THE TRUNK AND A BARN DESTROYED. MAXIMUM WINDS OF 105-110 MPH.

THIS WAS ONE OF TWO EF-1 TORNADOES NORTHEAST OF GRANT PARK. THIS HAD MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100-110 MPH AND A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH UP TO 150 YARDS. PATH ENDED ABOUT 5 MILES WEST SOUTHWEST OF LOWELL IN LAKE COUNTY INDIANA. MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE INCLUDED STAINED GLASS WINDOWS BLOWN OUT OF A CHURCH BY LARGE LIMBS...TREE BRANCH IMPALED INTO CHURCH AND A HOME...SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO A HOME UNDER CONSTRUCTION BUT SEALED...HARDWOODS AND SOFTWOODS SNAPPED AT THE TRUNK...AND EXTENSIVE CROP DAMAGE.
LARGE TREE LIMBS DOWN. ESTIMATED WINDS OF 85 MPH.

THIS WAS THE SECOND OF TWO EF-1 TORNADOES NORTHEAST OF GRANT PARK AND WAS SHOWN TO BE A SEPARATE DISTINCT PATH FROM THE OTHER. THIS TORNADO HAD A PATH OF 1.7 MILES AND ENDED VERY NEAR THE ILLINOIS AND INDIANA STATE LINE. DAMAGE INCLUDED A DESTROYED BARN AND HARDWOOD...SOFTWOOD TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED...AND EXTENSIVE CROP DAMAGE.

EF-1 TORNADO SOUTHEAST OF LOWELL HAD AN APPROXIMATE LENGTH OF 2.4 MILES AND A WIDTH OF UP TO 500 YARDS. MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE INCLUDED TWO SNAPPED WOODEN POWER POLES...SUBSTANTIALLY DAMAGED GRAIN BINS AND A DESTROYED WOODEN BARN. ALSO MULTIPLE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES.

EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS UP TO 100-105 MPH TOUCHED DOWN WEST OF DE MOTTE AND LIFTED ABOUT 5 MILES NORTHEAST OF DE MOTTE. CONSISTENT 100-200 YARD SWATH OF SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE THROUGH PATH...AS WELL AS TWO DESTROYED BARNS AND SOME STRUCTURAL/ROOF DAMAGE TO A DOUBLE WIDE MANUFACTURED HOME.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100-105 MPH. THIS TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF AROUND SIX MILES AND ENDED AROUND 2 MILES EAST-NORTHEAST OF DE MOTTE. CONSISTENT 100-200 YARD SWATH OF SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED THROUGH THE PATH AS WELL AS TWO DESTROYED BARNS AND SOME STRUCTURAL/ROOF DAMAGE TO A DOUBLE WIDE MANUFACTURED HOME. THIS WAS REVISED TO INCLUDE A SHORTER PATH LENGTH.
